rootCauseAnalysis.summary: Deployment api-gateway was patched to reference a bad ConfigMap (gateway-config-bad) containing an invalid directive ('invalid_directive: true'), causing all updated pods to crash immediately on startup with exit code 1. The valid ConfigMap 'gateway-config' still exists and the fix is to revert the volume reference and patch out the invalid directive.
causalChain: Signal: KubePodCrashLooping on pod api-gateway-5b4ccdb7df-q9z9c in demo-alert-storm — 4 restarts, CrashLoopBackOff
Why is the pod crashing? Container exits with code 1 within ~1 second: log shows '[emerg] invalid directive found in /etc/demo-http-server/config.yaml — aborting'
Why is the config file invalid? The config volume is mounted from ConfigMap 'gateway-config-bad', which contains 'invalid_directive: true' — an unrecognized field rejected by the application

Why is the Deployment using 'gateway-config-bad'? A kubectl-patch operation at 2026-05-04T01:28:18Z changed the Deployment (generation 1→2) to reference 'gateway-config-bad' instead of the valid 'gateway-config'

Root cause: Incorrect kubectl-patch applied to Deployment 'api-gateway' swapped the config volume reference to a deliberately misconfigured ConfigMap, causing all newly-rolled pods to fail immediately on startup

dueDiligence.targetAccuracy: Remediation target is Deployment/api-gateway because its spec.template.spec.volumes[config].configMap.name must be changed from 'gateway-config-bad' to 'gateway-config'. This is a precise, automated configuration change. The pod and ReplicaSet are downstream symptoms, not the configuration owner.
dueDiligence.proportionality: Remediation is a single field change on one Deployment (volume configMap name). This is the most targeted fix possible. 3 of 7 replicas are affected; the other 4 are healthy on the old ReplicaSet and will be preserved during the rolling update.
dueDiligence.scopeCompleteness: Investigated: crashing pod (describe, events, current+previous logs), owning ReplicaSet (implicit via Deployment), Deployment (spec, status, patch history), both ConfigMaps ('gateway-config-bad' and 'gateway-config'), and resource context for remediation history. Node conditions not deeply inspected but ruled out by 4 healthy pods on same node.
dueDiligence.causalCompleteness: Traced fully: CrashLoopBackOff → exit code 1 → invalid config directive in mounted file → ConfigMap 'gateway-config-bad' contains 'invalid_directive: true' → Deployment was patched to reference the bad ConfigMap. Cannot go deeper without knowing who issued the kubectl-patch (audit log not available).
dueDiligence.evidenceSufficiency: All claims are backed by tool output: pod describe (volume reference, restart count, exit code), pod logs (exact error message from both current and previous), ConfigMap describe (exact invalid_directive field), Deployment describe (generation 2, kubectl-patch manager, volume reference). No assumptions made. Audit logs for who issued the patch were not accessible.
dueDiligence.regressionAwareness: No prior remediation history available for Deployment/api-gateway (tier1 and tier2 history both empty). N/A — no regression risk from prior fixes.
dueDiligence.alternativeHypotheses: Considered: (1) OOMKill — ruled out: exit code is 1 (application error), not 137 (OOMKill); memory limits (64Mi) are generous for this workload. (2) Image pull failure — ruled out: events show 'Container image already present on machine'. (3) Node pressure — ruled out: 4 replicas on the same node are running healthy on the old ReplicaSet. (4) Missing secret/env — ruled out: only env var is CONFIG_PATH which is correctly set; the error is explicitly about the config file content.
dueDiligence.confidenceCalibration: 0.97: Starting at 1.0. -0.02 for not having audit log access to confirm the exact actor/intent of the kubectl-patch. -0.01 for not inspecting all 5 replicas (only the signaled pod was checked, but all pods share the same spec so the root cause is identical across all crashlooping replicas).

contributingFactors: A kubectl-patch operation at 2026-05-04T01:28:18Z changed the Deployment's volume reference from 'gateway-config' to 'gateway-config-bad'
ConfigMap 'gateway-config-bad' contains 'invalid_directive: true' which the demo-http-server application rejects as an unrecognized config key, causing exit code 1 within ~1 second of startup
The rolling update strategy (maxUnavailable: 25%) allowed 3 of 7 replicas to become unavailable before the crash loop was detected
The valid ConfigMap 'gateway-config' still exists in the namespace and is immediately usable as a fix

kaResponse.analysis: Deployment api-gateway was patched to reference a bad ConfigMap (gateway-config-bad) containing an invalid directive ('invalid_directive: true'), causing all updated pods to crash immediately on startup with exit code 1. The valid ConfigMap 'gateway-config' still exists and the fix is to revert the volume reference and patch out the invalid directive.
